name: provision-artifacts
description: Publish web artifacts (static sites or running apps) to a public subdomain
author: Provision AI

tools.name: artifact_publish
tools.description: Publish a web artifact from this server so it's reachable on the public internet at {your-slug}.provisionagents.com/{path}. Use for a static site/report (built HTML in this agent's public dir) or a running app (a long-lived process on a port). Re-publishing the same path updates it.

path_slug.description: URL path segment the artifact is served under (e.g. 'dashboard' → /dashboard/). Lowercase letters, digits, and hyphens. Defaults to a slug of the name.

type.description: 'static' serves files from this agent's public root; 'app' reverse-proxies a running process on an allocated port. Defaults to 'static'.

source_dir.description: Directory relative to this agent's public root (for example, 'leads' for public/leads). For apps it is the command's working directory and is required. For static artifacts it defaults to path_slug. Never pass an absolute path.

start_command.description: For app artifacts: the shell command run from source_dir. It must listen on the PORT env var provided to it. Required when type is 'app'; do not cd to an absolute path.

visibility.description: 'public' is open to anyone; 'gated' requires a secret ?token= link that's returned in public_url. Defaults to 'public'.

tools.name: artifact_list
tools.description: List the web artifacts you have published, with their public URLs and status.

tools.name: artifact_unpublish
tools.description: Take a published artifact offline: removes its route (and stops its process if it's an app).

artifact_id.description: The id of the artifact to unpublish (from artifact_list).
